params.reason: Animal brush making bristles or hair (heading 05.02) and horsehair or horsehair waste (heading 05.11) are excluded from the current chapter/section
Human hair or articles of human hair classified in headings 05.01, 67.03 or 67.04 are excluded, except filtering or straining cloth of heading 59.11
Cotton linters or other vegetable materials of Chapter 14 are excluded from this heading/section
Asbestos of heading 25.24 and articles of asbestos or other products of headings 68.12 and 68.13 are excluded from this section/chapter
Articles of headings 30.05, 30.06, and dental floss of heading 33.06 are excluded from this section
Sensitised textiles of headings 37.01 to 37.04 are excluded from this section

Monofilament with cross-sectional dimension exceeding 1 mm, or strip of width exceeding 5 mm made of plastics (Chapter 39), or plaits/fabrics/basketware made from such materials (Chapter 46) are excluded from this heading
Woven, knitted or crocheted fabrics, felt or nonwovens, impregnated, coated, covered or laminated with plastics, or articles thereof, of Chapter 39 are excluded
Chapter 59 Note: woven, knitted or crocheted fabrics, felt or nonwovens impregnated, coated, covered or laminated with rubber, or articles thereof of Chapter 40 are excluded
Hides or skins with hair/wool on (Chapters 41 or 43) or articles of furskin, artificial fur or articles thereof (headings 43.03 or 43.04) are excluded
Articles of textile materials of headings 42.01 or 42.02 are excluded
Products or articles of Chapter 48 (including cellulose wadding) are excluded from this section/chapter
Footwear, parts of footwear, gaiters, leggings and similar articles of Chapter 64 are excluded from this section/chapter
Hair-nets or other headgear or parts thereof of Chapter 65 are excluded from this section
Chapter 67 goods are excluded from this section/chapter
Abrasive-coated textile material (6805) and carbon fibres or articles of carbon fibres (6815) are excluded from the current heading/section
Glass fibres or articles of glass fibres, other than embroidery with glass thread on a visible ground of fabric, are excluded and fall under Chapter 70
Chapter 94 articles (furniture, bedding, luminaires and lighting fittings) are excluded from the current section/chapter
Articles of Chapter 95 (toys, games, sports requisites and nets) are excluded from the current section/chapter
Chapter 96 articles (brushes, travel sets for sewing, slide fasteners, typewriter ribbons, sanitary towels, tampons, napkins, napkin liners) are excluded
Articles of Chapter 97 are excluded from the current section/chapter
Goods of heading 62.12 are excluded from this section/chapter
Worn clothing or other worn articles of heading 63.09 are excluded

Chapter 61 Note: garments with pockets below waist, ribbed waistband, tightening at bottom, or less than 10 stitches per linear cm are excluded from headings 6105 and 6106; sleeveless garments excluded from 6105
Heading 61.09 Note: garments with drawstring, ribbed waistband or other means of tightening at the bottom are excluded from this heading

conditions.resolution: classify in heading 6111 when prima facie classifiable in both

params.rule: classify in heading 61.13 when prima facie classifiable in both 61.13 and other headings (excluding 61.11)

params.covers: cut otherwise than into squares or rectangles

params.meaning: articles produced in finished state, ready for use or merely needing separation by cutting dividing threads, without sewing or other working

params.covers: cut fabrics with at least one heat-sealed edge with visibly tapered or compressed border and other edges treated as described in Note
exclusions: fabrics with cut edges prevented from unravelling by hot cutting or other simple means

params.covers: Fabrics with hemmed or rolled edges, or with knotted fringe at edges

excludedConditions.value: cut edges prevented from unravelling by whipping or other simple means

params.heading: not_specified
params.covers: textiles cut to size and having undergone drawn thread work process
assemblies of two or more textiles joined by sewing, gumming or other methods

conditions.value: piece goods of identical material joined end to end
layered textiles whether or not padded

params.covers: knitted or crocheted articles made to shape

params.meaning: garments of headings 61.01 to 61.14 and headings 62.01 to 62.11

params.heading: Section XI
params.covers: textiles, garments and other textile articles incorporating chemical, mechanical or electronic components

conditions.value: retain essential character of Section XI goods

params.covers: made up knitted or crocheted articles
orthopaedic appliances, surgical belts, trusses or the like

params.meaning: a set of garments composed of two or three pieces made up in identical fabric, comprising a suit coat or jacket with four or more panels and a garment to cover the lower part of the body (trousers, breeches, shorts, skirt, or divided skirt). All components must be of the same fabric construction, colour, composition, style and corresponding/compatible size, though piping may be in different fabric. Includes morning dress, evening dress (tailcoat), and dinner jacket suits.

a set of garments (other than suits and articles of heading 61.07, 61.08 or 61.09), composed of several pieces made up in identical fabric, put up for retail sale, comprising one garment designed to cover the upper part of the body (with exceptions for pullovers and waistcoats in twin sets) and one or two different garments designed to cover the lower part of the body (trousers, bib and brace overalls, breeches, shorts, skirt or divided skirt). All components must be of the same fabric construction, style, colour and composition, and of corresponding or compatible size. Does not apply to track suits or ski suits of heading 61.12

meanings.shirts: garments designed to cover the upper part of the body, having long or short sleeves and a full or partial opening starting at the neckline
meanings.shirt-blouses: garments designed to cover the upper part of the body, having long or short sleeves and a full or partial opening starting at the neckline
meanings.blouses: loose-fitting garments designed to cover the upper part of the body, may be sleeveless and with or without an opening at the neckline

params.meaning: articles for young children of a body height not exceeding 86 cm
a one-piece garment designed to cover the upper and lower parts of the body; in addition to sleeves and a collar the ski overall may have pockets or footstraps

a set of garments composed of two or three pieces, put up for retail sale, comprising: one garment such as an anorak, wind-cheater, wind-jacket or similar article closed by a slide fastener (zipper), possibly with a waistcoat, and one pair of trousers, breeches, or bib and brace overall; or an overall with a padded sleeveless jacket. All components must be made of the same texture, style and composition, and of corresponding or compatible size

Garments designed for left over right closure at the front are regarded as men's or boys' garments; those designed for right over left closure at the front are regarded as women's or girls' garments. Exception: these provisions do not apply where the cut of the garment clearly indicates it is designed for one or other of the sexes.
